answer:
Sketch:
- LTV = $120 × 80% / 3% = $3,200
- LTV/CAC = 4x (good, >3x threshold)
- CAC payback = $800 / $96 = ~8.3 months (good, <12)
- But 3% monthly = ~31% annual logo churn (high)
- Profile reads SMB-focused, $1,440 ACV

Answer: Yes — fundable on unit economics. A 4x LTV/CAC and ~8-month payback both clear standard venture benchmarks (>3x, <12 months). The concern investors will raise is 31% annualized logo churn: at that leak rate, growth requires constant new-logo replacement and net revenue retention is almost certainly below 100%, which caps valuation multiples. To strengthen the pitch: show expansion revenue offsetting churn (target NRR ≥ 100%), cohort data proving churn concentrates in the first 1–2 months then flattens, or a path upmarket to stickier customers. Fundable now at seed/Series A; the churn story determines whether you get a good multiple or a discounted one.
